variation_instruction.prompt: Research the which component library fits best with our simple no-framework philosophy

llm_gate_results.reasoning: The implementation satisfies all explicit requirements: (1) plain HTML/vanilla JS with Web Components using Shadow DOM; (2) Shoelace as a lightweight UI component library; (3) no client-side routing; (4) reusable components (app-shell, app-card), utility functions (dom.js), and classes (Web Component classes); (5) placeholder tests via Vitest that run with 'npm run test'; (6) 'npm run dev' via Vite dev server; (7) .gitignore excluding node_modules/, dist/, .vite/; (8) no documentation or comments. The implementation also captures the underlying purpose — it creates a solid, extensible scaffold with proper DOM utility abstractions, component architecture, and tooling integration rather than just mechanically checking boxes.

All tests contain meaningful assertions that verify real behavior. dom-utils.test.js thoroughly tests qs, qsa, createElement, and emit with concrete expectations (toBeNull, toHaveLength, toBe, toEqual). app-card.test.js verifies shadow DOM creation, attribute/property reflection, and event emission with proper detail checking. No tests are trivially passing, no assertions are missing, and no existing tests appear to have been removed or weakened. Coverage gaps exist (app-shell.js and main.js untested) but the tests that do exist are honest and substantive.

Code review shows clean layout structure: app-shell uses flexbox header (space-between) with grid content area, app-card has properly styled card container with slot-based header/body/footer. CSS reset applied, max-width centered layout, Shoelace design tokens used throughout. No occlusion, overflow, misalignment, or broken layout patterns found. This is a seed scaffold with minimal, well-structured UI.
